title: JWST CO2 detection in the atmosphere of WASP-39b
abstract: We report a robust detection of carbon dioxide in the transmission spectrum of the hot Saturn WASP-39b using JWST NIRSpec. The 4.3 micron CO2 feature constrains the atmospheric metallicity and carbon-to-oxygen ratio.

title: Photochemically produced sulfur dioxide on WASP-39b
abstract: Follow-up analysis of WASP-39b reveals sulfur dioxide, a photochemical product, alongside water and carbon monoxide. The detection implies active photochemistry in this hot giant exoplanet atmosphere.

title: Carbon-bearing molecules and a possible biosignature on K2-18b
abstract: Transmission spectroscopy of the habitable-zone sub-Neptune K2-18b shows methane and carbon dioxide, with a tentative dimethyl sulfide signal. We discuss implications for a possible Hycean world.

title: Search for a terrestrial atmosphere on TRAPPIST-1e
abstract: We present transmission observations of the temperate rocky planet TRAPPIST-1e. The flat spectrum disfavors a cloud-free hydrogen-dominated atmosphere but cannot rule out a high-mean-molecular-weight secondary atmosphere.

title: Thermal emission from TRAPPIST-1b consistent with bare rock
abstract: Secondary-eclipse photometry of TRAPPIST-1b yields a high dayside brightness temperature consistent with a dark bare rock and little to no atmosphere.

title: Sodium and high-altitude haze in HD 189733b
abstract: High-resolution spectroscopy of the hot Jupiter HD 189733b detects neutral sodium absorption and strong Rayleigh scattering from a high-altitude haze layer.

title: Water vapor and atmospheric escape from HD 209458b
abstract: We measure water vapor in the atmosphere of HD 209458b and characterize an extended, escaping hydrogen envelope driven by stellar irradiation.

title: A tenuous outgassed atmosphere on the lava world 55 Cancri e
abstract: JWST thermal measurements of the ultra-short-period super-Earth 55 Cancri e are consistent with a secondary atmosphere outgassed from a magma ocean, rich in CO and CO2.

title: A high-metallicity, hazy atmosphere on GJ 1214b
abstract: The mini-Neptune GJ 1214b shows a flat, featureless transmission spectrum indicating a high-metallicity atmosphere muted by high-altitude clouds or haze.

title: Thermal inversion and metal emission in WASP-121b
abstract: Emission spectroscopy of the ultra-hot Jupiter WASP-121b reveals a temperature inversion and emission features from gaseous metals on the dayside.

title: Reflective clouds on the ultra-hot Neptune LTT 9779b
abstract: Phase-curve photometry of LTT 9779b indicates a high geometric albedo produced by reflective silicate clouds on the dayside of this ultra-hot Neptune.

title: A cloud-free water detection in WASP-96b with JWST NIRISS
abstract: JWST NIRISS transmission spectroscopy of the hot Saturn WASP-96b shows a clear water absorption signature with little evidence for clouds.

title: Photochemical modeling of sulfur species in irradiated exoplanets
abstract: We develop a photochemical model predicting sulfur dioxide production in hot giant exoplanet atmospheres under intense stellar UV irradiation.

title: Bayesian atmospheric retrieval methods for transmission spectra
abstract: We describe a free-chemistry Bayesian retrieval framework for inferring molecular abundances and temperature structure from exoplanet transmission spectra.
